description: Add a local `hal factory run` executor that wraps the existing `hal auto` compound pipeline, creates and updates durable factory run records, records timeline events and artifacts, and emits a stable `factory-run-v1` JSON result without changing existing `hal auto` behavior.

userStories.title: Lock factory-run-v1 result contract
userStories.description: As a supervisor integration, I want the factory run command result schema locked so that automated callers can parse local run outcomes safely.
acceptanceCriteria: The command JSON result includes contract identity for `factory-run-v1`.
The command JSON result includes version, run ID, status, next action, artifact references, event summary, and failure details fields.
Failure fields include classification, error message, and suggested command when available.
A contract document or example payload documents every emitted JSON field.
Tests assert the JSON key names for the command result contract.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add factory run command input parsing
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `hal factory run` to accept supported local entry options so that I can start a run from a PRD markdown file or analysis report.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory run` accepts at most one positional PRD markdown path.
`hal factory run --report <path>` accepts a report path without requiring a positional path.
Passing both a positional PRD path and `--report` returns a validation error before creating a run record.
Passing more than one positional argument returns a validation error before creating a run record.
`--base <branch>` is accepted by the command parser.
`--json` is accepted by the command parser.
Command help documents the positional path, `--report`, `--base`, and `--json`.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create durable run record before execution
userStories.description: As a supervisor, I want a factory run record created before pipeline execution so that every attempted local run has durable state.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory run <markdown-prd>` creates a run record before invoking the local pipeline dependency.
`hal factory run --report <path>` creates a run record before invoking the local pipeline dependency.
The run record stores local executor mode.
The run record stores the original entry source path and source type.
The run record is updated to an in-progress state before pipeline execution begins.
Unit tests verify creation and state transition through an injected `internal/factory.Store`.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Invoke existing compound pipeline
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the factory run executor to call existing compound pipeline behavior through injected dependencies so that it stays aligned with `hal auto`.
acceptanceCriteria: The factory executor calls existing `internal/compound` pipeline entry points or command-level helpers instead of reimplementing pipeline steps.
Markdown PRD path entry is passed to the wrapped pipeline in the same semantic form used by `hal auto`.
Report-driven entry is passed to the wrapped pipeline in the same semantic form used by `hal auto`.
`--base <branch>` is propagated into the wrapped pipeline options when provided.
Unit tests use dependency stubs and do not invoke real engines, Codex, GitHub CLIs, network calls, or long-running local pipeline calls.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record local run timeline events
userStories.description: As a supervisor, I want local run lifecycle events written to the factory timeline so that I can inspect what happened during execution.
acceptanceCriteria: A timeline event is recorded when the run starts.
A timeline event is recorded before invoking the local compound pipeline.
Progress events are recorded when existing wrapped pipeline hooks expose progress information.
A completion event is recorded when the wrapped pipeline succeeds.
A failure event is recorded when the wrapped pipeline returns an error.
Tests verify event ordering for success and failure paths.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record run artifact references
userStories.description: As a supervisor, I want relevant artifact paths captured on the run record so that generated state can be inspected after completion or failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs started from markdown record the source PRD markdown path.
Runs started from `--report` record the report path.
The run record includes canonical `.hal/prd.json` when it exists.
The run record includes generated report, review, CI, PR, or related artifact paths when available through existing pipeline state.
The run record includes the factory run record path itself.
Missing optional artifacts do not cause an otherwise successful run to fail.
Tests verify artifact references for markdown and report entry flows.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist successful run status
userStories.description: As a supervisor, I want successful local runs to persist final status and next action so that follow-up automation can decide what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: Successful wrapped pipeline completion updates the run record to a terminal success status.
The final result reports the same status as the durable run record.
The final result includes a next action derived from available run state.
The event summary in the final result reflects the recorded timeline events.
Tests verify successful run status, next action, artifacts, and event summary.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist failed run details
userStories.description: As a supervisor, I want failed local runs to persist classification and recovery guidance so that failures are actionable after the process exits.
acceptanceCriteria: Wrapped pipeline errors update the run record to a terminal failure status.
Failure state includes the error message returned by the wrapped pipeline.
Failure state includes a deterministic classification of validation, pipeline, engine, git, CI, or unknown based on available error context.
Failure classification uses `unknown` when available context is insufficient.
Failure state includes a suggested inspection or resume command when one can be safely derived.
Tests verify failed execution persists status, classification, error message, artifacts, and suggested command.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Emit JSON factory run result
userStories.description: As a supervisor integration, I want `hal factory run --json` to emit the persisted run result so that machine workflows can inspect outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: `hal factory run <markdown-prd> --json` emits a valid `factory-run-v1` payload.
`hal factory run --report <path> --json` emits the same `factory-run-v1` result shape.
The JSON output includes run ID, status, next action, artifact references, and event summary.
Failure JSON output includes the same classification, error message, and suggested command persisted in the run record.
Tests assert success and failure JSON output fields for markdown and report entry flows.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render non-JSON factory run summary
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want `hal factory run` without `--json` to print a concise summary so that local usage remains understandable.
acceptanceCriteria: Non-JSON success output includes run ID, final status, and next action.
Non-JSON failure output includes run ID, final status, error summary, and suggested command when available.
Human output is produced by `hal factory run` and does not alter `hal auto` output.
Tests assert key human output phrases for success and failure.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve hal auto behavior
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the factory run wrapper isolated from direct `hal auto` execution so that existing users are not affected.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing `hal auto` public flags are unchanged.
Existing `hal auto` argument validation is unchanged.
Existing `hal auto --json` output shape is unchanged.
Existing `hal auto` text output is unchanged.
Shared code extracted for factory reuse remains behaviorally compatible with existing `hal auto` tests.
Regression tests cover the factory wrapper separately from direct `hal auto` invocation.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
